title: Troximon lanatum
intro: Troximon lanatum, also known as Woolly Groundsel, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Troximon lanatum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used in folk medicine and traditional remedies.
taxonomy: Troximon lanatum is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This family comprises of flowering plants with daisy-like or sunflower-like appearances. The genus Troximon is a small group of flowering plants, and Troximon lanatum is one of its species. It is native to the Mediterranean region. Within the Asteraceae family, Troximon lanatum belongs to the order Asterales.
